summary: The diff adds `frontend/src/components/TimeControls.tsx` as a new standalone React component with props for time state and handlers, and exports a default `TimeControls` component. `InteractiveGlobe.tsx` is not present in the modified files list. The component covers Real/Hourly/Yearly mode controls and a time display. However, the spec requires encapsulating 'the time button (the button that opens/toggles the time picker or time mode)' — the diff's props interface and rendered output don't clearly include a dedicated time-picker toggle button. Also, the spec says props should mirror what is currently used in InteractiveGlobe.tsx, but the diff uses a different prop shape that may or may not match.

missing_requirements.requirement: The component must encapsulate the time button (the button that opens/toggles the time picker or time mode)
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows controls for Real/Hourly/Yearly animation modes and a clock display, but no explicit time button that opens or toggles a time picker is visible in the component code. The spec specifically calls out this button as a required element.

hallucinated_features.description: Custom prop shape using formatHourlyDate and formatYearlyDate callbacks passed as props
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec requires props mirroring what is currently used in InteractiveGlobe.tsx (citing examples like timeMode, onTimeModeChange, currentTime, onTimeChange). Passing date-formatting functions as props is an abstraction not mentioned in the spec and not evidenced as matching the existing InteractiveGlobe.tsx interface.
